chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P No.S-365 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 20-10-2025 Mr. Muzafar Ali Wadho, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Aftab Nek Muhammad Bhutto, Assistant A.G a/w ASI Nisar Ahmed of PS Waleed, SIP Ali Dost on behalf of SSP, Larkana and Inspector Dili Jan Magsi, SHO, PS Mahotta. Mr. Zain-ul-Abideen Abbasi, A.P.G for the State. ************** Mr. Ajmair Ali Bhutto, Advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.5 and 6, along with objections. Copy of the same is supplied to the learned counsel for the petitioner as well as to the learned Assistant Advocate General (A.A.G.). The learned A.A.G. has also filed statements/comments on behalf of respondents No.2 to 4, which are hereby taken on record. In their respective statements, respondents No.3 and 4 have denied the allegations levelled against them, stating that they have never harassed the petitioner nor do they intend to do so. They further undertake that they shall act strictly in accordance with law. The learned A.A.G. submits that all official respondents are legally bound to act in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioner, if and when required. Upon perusal of the petition, it appears that the controversy between the parties primarily pertains to a civil dispute. It is well settled that this Court, in its constitutional jurisdiction, is not competent to adjudicate disputed questions of fact or to resolve civil disputes between private parties. The petitioner and/or private respondents, if aggrieved by any act or omission arising out of such civil dispute, may avail appropriate remedy before the competent forum having jurisdiction, in accordance with law.

It is well settled that this Court, in its constitutional jurisdiction, is not competent to adjudicate disputed questions of fact or to resolve civil disputes between private parties. The petitioner and/or private respondents, if aggrieved by any act or omission arising out of such civil dispute, may avail appropriate remedy before the competent forum having jurisdiction, in accordance with law. In view of the above, the instant petition stands disposed of, with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner and to provide her protection as and when required. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, for information and necessary compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
